meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve No SaaS Knowledge
content.seo_description: Overcome the lack of SaaS knowledge with this student-focused starter kit. Master project building using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your career.
content.answer_block: The SaaS starter kit is your bridge from student to developer. By leveraging Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for implementation, you bypass the complexity of infrastructure. This curated approach turns abstract concepts into a deployable portfolio piece, giving you the professional confidence to launch your first real-world application.
content.intro:
The blank editor is a student's greatest enemy. You have the ambition, but the chasm between 'learning to code' and 'shipping a SaaS' feels insurmountable. You’re drowning in tutorials that teach syntax but ignore the reality of authentication, database schemas, and payment gateways.

But the game has changed. You no longer need to build everything from scratch to prove your worth. By using a robust SaaS starter kit, you are standing on the shoulders of giants. We are stripping away the noise, focusing on high-impact architecture that turns your student project into a production-ready engine.

steps.title: Define the MVP Scope
steps.description: Establish the core value proposition of your SaaS. Keep it focused to avoid scope creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Generate a lean SaaS project scope for a student portfolio app that solves a specific university productivity problem. Include a feature list and a database schema for user auth.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'generate project requirements.md based on the architecture plan'

steps.title: Bootstrap with Starter Kit
steps.description: Initialize your project using a modern, opinionated SaaS boilerplate.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a Next.js 14+ project with Tailwind CSS, Supabase for auth, and Stripe integration. Ensure the file structure follows industry best practices for scalability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'initialize project structure and install dependencies'

steps.title: Build the Authentication Layer
steps.description: Implement secure user sign-in and protected routes to safeguard your app.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a middleware-based authentication flow that protects dashboard routes and handles user session persistence using Supabase Auth.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'implement auth middleware and login page'

steps.title: Develop the Core Feature
steps.description: Focus on the primary utility that makes your SaaS valuable to users.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a CRUD interface for the core productivity tool defined in step 1. Ensure the UI is responsive and data is correctly synced to the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'implement core feature dashboard component'

steps.title: Ship and Deploy
steps.description: Automate the deployment pipeline to get your app live on the internet.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Vercel configuration file and a deployment checklist for a production-ready SaaS application.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'configure production environment variables and prepare for deployment'

pro_tips: Prioritize 'Time to First Commit' over perfect architecture; you can refactor later, but you can't refactor a project that never started.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your API endpoints before you write the UI; it enforces contract-first development.
Never build custom OAuth systems; use Supabase or Clerk to offload security risks to professionals.
Treat your GitHub commit history as your resume—make every commit message descriptive and meaningful.
Focus on one 'killer feature' that solves a specific pain point rather than trying to build a platform that does everything.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, focus on gathering feedback from at least 10 real users to iterate on your product features and refine your database schema.
content.conversion_block: You are closer to launching than you think. Stop waiting for the perfect moment or the perfect skill level. The tools exist, the path is clear, and your portfolio is waiting. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
